description_long: This is a streamlined, vintage-style airplane with a predominantly orange fuselage and beige wings. The aircraft features a single propeller mounted at the nose, with a sleek, pointed spinner and a two-blade propeller tipped in yellow. The wings are low-mounted and straight, with rounded tips and a subtle dihedral angle, while the tail section includes a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The cockpit is situated just behind the wing's leading edge and appears to be a single-seat, enclosed design with a clear canopy. The landing gear is a taildragger configuration, with two main wheels under the wings and a small tail wheel. Distinctive markings, including a roundel and a flag on the tail, add to its classic appearance.
description: A vintage-style orange airplane with beige low-mounted wings, a single nose propeller, and a taildragger landing gear. It features a single-seat enclosed cockpit and classic aviation markings.
description_view_0: Side view showing the orange fuselage, beige low-mounted wings, single propeller at the nose, and taildragger landing gear.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the full wingspan, cockpit placement, and the symmetrical layout of the wings and tail.
description_view_2: Opposite side view similar to view 0, showing the propeller, landing gear, and tail details from the other side.
description_view_3: Bottom-up view displaying the underside of the wings, propeller, and the taildragger landing gear configuration.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, straight wings with rounded tips and a slight dihedral angle; no winglets or variable geometry.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two main fixed wheels and a single tail wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, single-seat enclosed cockpit with a clear canopy.
